studies.title: Randomized controlled trial to evaluate the effectiveness of a digital health application (Kaia) to prevent back pain and improve quality of life in hospital staff
studies.health_condition:
ICD-10-GM (translation):
M54 - Dorsalgia
Healthy volunteers:
Yes
studies.brief_summary: Back pain often leads to sick leave among nursing staff in Germany. Digital health apps (DiGA) could help to reduce these problems. These are tested mobile phone apps for health that doctors can prescribe.  Kaia is a fitness app with exercises for chronic back pain. There is still little research on how such apps can help nurses with back pain in the workplace. A new study wants to find out whether the Kaia app works better against back pain than normal preventive measures. The study will also look at whether the nurses' sleep, mood and general fitness improve. The aim is to find a simple digital solution to a common problem in the nursing profession.
studies.intervention:
Arm 1:
Intervention group: Subjects receive the DiGA Kaia Health over 12 weeks. Exercises should be performed daily for between 10-30 minutes.
Arm 2:
Control group: standard prevention for back pain
Endpoints

studies.brief_summary: Kaia Back Pain, a software application for the treatment of non-specific back pain that includes digital delivery of patient-centred content of multidisciplinary rehabilitation.
studies.intervention:
Arm 1:
Control group gets usual care 
Arm 2:
Intervention group gets usual care and uses Kaia Back Pain
Endpoints

studies.title: The Kaia COPD software Application:  a digital therapeutic delivering PR to symptomatic COPD patients for self-management in the home setting – a randomized, controlled, multicentered and multinational clinical study.
studies.health_condition:
ICD-10-GM (translation):
J44 - Other chronic obstructive pulmonary disease
Healthy volunteers:
No Entry
studies.brief_summary: With this study, we would like to investigate whether the use of a smartphone application (Kaia COPD) can improve the burden of symptoms and physical performance in people suffering from chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD). Furthermore, we are interested in the effects on various factors such as quality of life, emotional state and health status as well as health care utilisation. For this purpose, we compare people who use the Kaia COPD app at home for half a year with people who do not use the Kaia COPD app and receive standard treatment.
studies.intervention:
Arm 1:
Kaia COPD software application for self-management of pulmonary rehabilitation, on 3-7 days per week
Arm 2:
Active control group: physical activity,  a brochure “Besser leben mit COPD”, including written behavioural and exercise recommendations
Endpoints

studies.title: e-Health and mobile health application for the self-management support of patients with low back pain - a confirmatory trial
studies.health_condition:
ICD-10-GM (translation):
M54 - Dorsalgia
Healthy volunteers:
No Entry
studies.brief_summary: Low back pain (LBP) is one of the leading causes for global disability and has been understood as a multidimensional disorder. In this trial, we aim to investigate the treatment effects of a medical multiplatform application (Kaia) by comparing kaia treatment to a control goup.  Patients in the control group receive individual physical therapy carried out by a certified therapist and online education. After 6 and 12 weeks, patients fill out questionnaires to assess between-group differences regarding pain intensity, functional capability and health-related quality of life.
studies.intervention:
Arm 1:
Treatment by kaia LBP app throughout the whole Trial period of 6 months, self-administered by patients on own mobile device, patients are encouraged to use app at least four times a week
Arm 2:
Patients in the control group receive standard physical therapy carried out by a certified therapist at an affiliated center for pyhsical exercises once a week (in total 6 sessions). In addition, patients are sent weekly email featuring links to online education resources regarding LBP (in total 6

classification.reason: The study involves a comparison between a treatment group (Kaia application) and a control group (physical therapy and online education), which strongly suggests a controlled trial. The use of 'confirmatory trial' and 'between-group differences' implies a prospective design. However, randomization is not explicitly mentioned, so confidence is slightly reduced.

studies.title: Impact of a Smartphone application (KAIA COPD-App) in combination with Activity Monitoring as maintenance program following pulmonary rehabilitation in COPD : an international multi-centered randomised controlled trial
studies.health_condition:
ICD-10-GM (translation):
J44 - Other chronic obstructive pulmonary disease
Healthy volunteers:
No
studies.brief_summary: The purpose of this study is to investigate how the use of a smartphone application (COPD-APP) in combination with activity monitoring in patients with chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD) affects physical activity, quality of life, physical fitness, symptoms, feeling and health. For this, we compare people who carry out the program at home for half a year (training group) with persons who perform only an activity monitoring and regular tests (control group).
studies.intervention:
Arm 1:
Device : KAIA COPD-App (Mobile Application).
The study intervention is an exercise training program that requires a chair and elastic bands, consisting of training elements with progressive levels of intensity, individually adaptable to the participant’s exercise level. This training program is delivered to the participants with the help of KAIA COPD-App. The individualized training sessions will last approximately 15-20 minutes and will be conducted by the participants seven days per wee
